title: brain quarantine inspect <opaqueId> [--reveal] [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: PRIVILEGED operator command to inspect a quarantined item (bootstrap-migration.md §7). EVERY invocation is challenge-bound: the quarantine registry is privileged classification, so even a metadata-only inspection requires an OS-mediated presence assertion bound to the broker challenge (op quarantine inspect, mechanism os-presence per security-broker-contract.md §7.4/§7.5). Presence is asserted via THREE flows: (1) the interactive OS-presence prompt bound to the challenge in-process (the default — no --authorization needed), (2) the --export-challenge → sign → --authorization out-of-band flow, or (3) a supplied --authorization; --yes never authorizes. The challenge's intendedEffect binds the quarantine item opaque id AND intendedEffect.revealPurpose (metadata|reveal); the caller identity and the vault are bound by the os-presence mechanism itself (the per-vault broker instance + the trusted-CLI local identity) — so an authorization can neither cross items/vaults/callers nor escalate metadata→reveal. Least-privilege by default: a metadata-purpose authorization emits metadata only (category, source path, detection rule) and NEVER the plaintext. Full content is shown ONLY when --reveal binds intendedEffect.revealPurpose=reveal, which for detected-credential items decrypts the AEAD quarantine store; a metadata-purpose authorization does NOT authorize a reveal (rejected authz.presence_unverified). Every invocation (and every reveal) writes an audit record. Exit 6 only when presence is unavailable/declined (and no --authorization) or a challenge is exported with --export-challenge (which emits an AuthorizationChallenge instead of this object). Refused items (unsupported-schema-version, unknown-type) are NOT quarantined and are never inspectable here.

opaqueId.description: Stable opaque id of the quarantined item.

revealed.description: True only when authorized --reveal returned full content; false for the default metadata-only inspection.

path.description: Repo-relative path of the source note/file.

detectedAt.description: Which graduation step quarantined the item.

rule.description: Detection rule id (for detected-credential); never the matched bytes.

content.description: Full plaintext content; present ONLY when revealed is true (authorized reveal).

examples.content:
---
id: shared-x
type: note
---

# Explicit A

x-atlas-contract.summary: Privileged, presence-authorized inspection of a quarantined item; metadata by default, full content only under an audited --reveal.

flags.description: Return full content (decrypts detected-credential AEAD). Binds intendedEffect.revealPurpose=reveal into the challenge; a metadata-purpose authorization does not authorize a reveal (authz.presence_unverified). Writes an audit record.

Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op quarantine inspect, os-presence; intendedEffect.revealPurpose=reveal when --reveal is set, else metadata) to stdout and exit 6 instead of inspecting. Omitting it uses the interactive OS-presence flow.

Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse from a prior --export-challenge; presented to the broker to authorize the inspection (reveal-bound when --reveal is set). Optional when the interactive OS-presence flow satisfies the challenge in-process.

sideEffects: on any authorized invocation: writes an audit record of the inspection (allowlisted metadata; never the plaintext)
on authorized --reveal: writes an additional reveal audit record
prohibitedEffects: never returns ANY output (not even metadata) without a valid presence-bound authorization — every invocation is challenge-bound (interactive presence, exported challenge, or supplied --authorization)
never emits plaintext without a reveal-bound authorization (intendedEffect.revealPurpose=reveal); a metadata-purpose authorization is insufficient (authz.presence_unverified)
never lets an authorization cross items, vaults, or callers — the item is bound in intendedEffect and the caller + vault are bound by the os-presence mechanism
never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge / OS presence assertion
never inspects a refused (unsupported-schema-version / unknown-type) item — refusals are not quarantined
never mutates the quarantined item (inspection only)

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, missing <opaqueId>, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
the interactive OS-presence assertion is unavailable or declined and no --authorization is supplied (metadata or --reveal), or a challenge was exported with --export-challenge
broker: the OS presence assertion is absent or not bound to the challenge, or a --reveal presented a metadata-purpose authorization (intendedEffect.revealPurpose=metadata) not bound to reveal intent
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: the quarantine item opaque id is not found / not quarantined
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
broker: the quarantine AEAD key is not readable by this trusted-CLI identity
broker: the signerId is not in the signer registry (SP-3: os-presence implemented as a presence-gated signature, §7.1)
broker: a non-presence signer is refused here — only a presence:true p256 signer may hold quarantine ops (§7.1)
broker: the presence-gated signature failed verification (or its prefix disagrees with the enrolled alg)
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
